content_body:
Fishing in Blue Ridge, GA, is a top activity for both locals and visitors, offering opportunities for trout fishing, fly fishing, and lake fishing. Here's a helpful breakdown of what to know:

Best Fishing Spots

  • Toccoa River (Tailwater & Upper Section) – Known for trophy trout, excellent fly fishing.
  • Lake Blue Ridge – Great for smallmouth bass, walleye, and bluegill.
  • Noontootla Creek – A smaller stream for wild trout fishing.
  • Rock Creek & Cooper Creek – Good for stocked rainbow and brown trout, ideal for families.

Fishing Regulations & Licenses

  • Georgia Fishing License Required – Obtainable online through Georgia DNR.
  • Special Trout License – Needed for trout waters.
  • Catch & Release Areas – Some stretches of the Toccoa River and Noontootla Creek require artificial lures only.

Best Fishing Seasons

  • Spring & Fall – Peak trout fishing months.
  • Winter (Tailwater Fishing) – The Toccoa River remains fishable year-round due to dam-controlled water temperatures.
  • Summer (Lake Fishing) – Best for bass and walleye on Lake Blue Ridge.

Fishing Techniques

  • Fly Fishing – Most popular on the Toccoa River and Noontootla Creek.
  • Spin Fishing – Effective for trout and bass in lakes and rivers.
  • Live Bait vs. Artificial – Some areas allow only artificial lures, while others permit live bait.

Guided Fishing Trips

Several outfitters provide guided fishing experiences:

  • Float North Georgia – Specializes in guided fly fishing trips.
  • Toccoa River Outfitters – Offers trophy trout fishing experiences.
  • Blue Ridge Fly Fishing – Provides lessons and full-day guided trips.

Where to Get Fishing Gear & Supplies

  • Toccoa River Outfitters – Fly fishing gear and local knowledge.
  • Blue Ridge Fly Fishing – Full-service shop for gear and guided trips.
  • Walmart (Blue Ridge) – Basic fishing supplies.

title: Fishing in Blue Ridge GA
